title: GrokRxiv Lean Faithfulness Verdict
description: Advisory paper-theorem <-> kernel-proved Lean-statement equivalence verdict. ADVISORY ONLY: never blocks publication; the Lean kernel remains the sole proof authority and a human is the final arbiter of faithfulness.

lean_declaration.description: The exact Lean declaration name of the kernel-proved target this verdict is about.

back_translation.description: A faithful natural-language rendering of the Lean statement as written, produced WITHOUT consulting the paper theorem text (round-trip back-translation). Render only what the Lean binders, hypotheses, and conclusion actually say.

hypothesis_completeness.description: Whether every hypothesis present in the paper theorem appears as a Lean binder or assumption.

complete.description: True only if no paper hypothesis is dropped, weakened, or collapsed (e.g. a typed relation reduced to an opaque predicate) in the Lean statement.

missing_hypotheses.description: Each paper hypothesis that is missing from or weaker than the Lean statement. Empty when complete is true.

conclusion_matches.description: Whether the Lean conclusion states the same proposition the paper theorem concludes.

triviality_risk.description: Whether the Lean statement is a trivially-true strawman (e.g. vacuous hypotheses, a degenerate special case, or a tautology) that the kernel would accept without capturing the paper's claim.

verdict.description: Overall advisory equivalence verdict. `faithful`: Lean statement captures the paper theorem; `suspect`: plausible but with unresolved divergences; `unfaithful`: drops a hypothesis, mismatches the conclusion, or is a strawman.

issues.description: Specific divergences found between the paper theorem and the Lean statement.
